entries.label: Payback core formula
entries.reason: scheduler applies baseRatio, regimeMultiplier, volMultiplier, minimum threshold, cost cap, and annual/per-period caps

entries.label: Payback KPI formulas
entries.reason: accumulator computes BYR, CG, TBR, roundTripEfficiency, and daysToBreakeven in sats-first form

entries.label: Leverage safety thresholds
entries.reason: healthFactorMin and liquidationBufferPct are validated and enforced in runtime gates

entries.label: Profit floor and variance gate
entries.reason: positive-edge logic exists, but default runtime floors are zero and the stricter doc stance is not encoded as default config

entries.label: BTC-denominated strategy accounting
entries.reason: payback and KPI accounting are sats-first, but route/loop-level BTC ±50% scenario coverage is not surfaced as a recurring audit artifact

entries.label: Kelly-based parameterization
entries.reason: Kelly is used as rationale for static config defaults, not as a live or periodic optimizer

entries.label: Advanced overfit statistics
entries.reason: audit:overfit now includes reporting-only DSR proxy, CSCV/PBO, and WFE sections, but these statistics are not live gates

mismatches.reason: Default risk policy still uses minNetProfitUsd=0 and minNetProfitPct=0.
No recurring artifact was found for BTC +/-50% strategy-level scenario audit.
Kelly-derived logic informs config comments but is not re-estimated from BobClaw evidence.

notes: Implemented means a concrete runtime or persisted reporting calculation exists in repo code.
Partial means the formula or principle influences config/rationale or some surfaces, but not the full recurring audit loop described in docs.
Missing means the docs call for the formula family, but current runtime/reporting code does not compute it directly.

notes: Mechanical simulation uses RPC estimation and eth_call only; it is not realized execution proof.
Pre-live execution audit requires plan, submission, receipt, and journal records to stay in sync.
Fork execution requires an external signer and never stores private keys in planner or dashboard code.
liveTrading is reported from deterministic policy gates; dashboard code never signs or authorizes transactions.
